meal_name: Mediterranean Omelet
ingredients_string: Basil:3 | Bell Pepper (Red):30 | Black Pepper:3 | Egg:60 | Kalamata Olives:15 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):10 | Parsley:5 | Sea Salt:5 | Shallots:20 | Thyme (Fresh):2 | Tomato (Raw):30
short_description: 150 kcal: Earthy and rich in B-vitamins for morning mental clarity.
instructions:
Prep the Eggs: Whisk the 100g-110g of eggs in a bowl. Finely chop your parsley and basil; stir half of the herbs into the eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Sauté the Spinach: Heat the 14g of olive oil in a medium non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add the 15g of baby spinach and sauté for about 30–45 seconds until wilted. Spread the spinach evenly across the pan.
Pour the Base: Pour the egg mixture over the spinach. Tilt the pan to ensure the eggs reach all edges. Let it cook for about 1–2 minutes until the bottom is golden and the top is mostly set but still slightly tacky.
Add the Fillings: On one half of the omelet, layer the following:
30g Roasted Red Peppers
30g Fresh Tomatoes (patted dry to avoid sogginess)
15g Kalamata Olives
20g-25g Goat Cheese
Fold and Steam: Gently fold the empty half of the omelet over the filling. Turn the heat to low, cover the pan with a lid for about 30–60 seconds. This creates a small amount of steam to soften the goat cheese and finish the eggs without overcooking the bottom.
Garnish: Slide the omelet onto a plate. Top with the remaining fresh herbs and, if you like, a crack of fresh black pepper.

meal_name: Cacao & Almond Chia
ingredients_string: Almond Butter:5 | Cacao Powder:5 | Coconut Chia Pudding (Base):100
short_description: 150 kcal: Magnesium-rich breakfast to satisfy cravings and support mood.
instructions: Mix seeds, milk, and cacao. Top with almond butter and sugar.

meal_name: Smashed Beans & Eggs
ingredients_string: Cannellini Beans:100
instructions:
Cozy up with these indulgent breakfast beans—creamy, cheesy, a little spicy, and topped with the perfect crispy fried egg.
Sauté the Aromatics: Heat the 54g of olive oil in a large skillet (stainless steel or cast iron works best) over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5–7 minutes until soft and translucent. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Simmer & Mash: Add the 525g of white beans. Season with a generous pinch of kosher salt and black pepper. If the beans seem too dry, add 2–3 tablespoons of water or broth. Use the back of a wooden spoon to lightly smash about one-third of the beans to create a creamy texture.
Finish with Cheese: Stir in the parmesan cheese until it melts into the beans, creating a glossy, savory sauce. Turn the heat to the lowest setting to keep warm.
Fry the Eggs
Crispy Edges: In a separate small skillet, heat the 40g of olive oil over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Cook: Crack the 4 eggs into the oil. Season with salt and pepper. Spoon some of the hot oil over the egg whites to help them set quickly while keeping the yolks runny.
Finish: Cook for 2–3 minutes until the edges are crispy and golden, but the yolks are still liquid.

meal_name: Coconut Chicken Curry
ingredients_string: Chicken thigh:500 | Coconut Milk:400 | Fish Sauce:15 | Jasmine Rice:200 | Palm sugar:8 | Thai basil:10
instructions: Rich red curry with coconut milk and tender chicken; serve over jasmine rice with fresh Thai basil.

meal_name: Crispy Chicken Tikka bowl w Quinoa & Mint Sauce
ingredients_string: Cashew Cream (Base):15 | Cashew Nuts:10 | Chicken Breast:90 | Chili Flakes:1 | Coriander Seeds:1 | Cucumber:30 | Cumin Seeds:1 | Fresh Coriander:5 | Fresh Mint:5 | Garam Masala:2 | Garlic (Raw):6 | Ginger (Raw):4 | Jalapeño:3 | Lime Juice:5 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):2 | Pickled Red Onion (Base):20 | Quinoa (White):30 | Sea Salt:2 | Slaw (Base):50 | Tomato (Raw):60 | White Onion:40
instructions:
Sauté the chopped white onion in 1g of olive oil for 3 minutes until softened. Stir in half the minced garlic and half the ginger, cooking for 1 minute.
Add the diced tomatoes, garam masala, ground coriander, cumin, chili flakes, raw cashews, and a splash of water. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes before blending until silky smooth.
Cook the quinoa according to package instructions and set aside.
Coat the cubed chicken breast in 1g of olive oil and a spoonful of the spiced tikka sauce. Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes, brush with extra tikka sauce, and return to the oven for 5 minutes until crispy.
Prepare the mint sauce by blending the mint leaves, cilantro, Cashew Cream (Base), remaining garlic, jalapeño, lime juice, and sea salt until smooth.
Assemble the bowl by layering the cooked quinoa, crispy chicken tikka, fresh slaw, sliced cucumbers, and pickled red onions, then drizzle the cashew mint sauce over the top.

meal_name: Leek & Salmon in Capers, Lemon & Dill Sauce w Roasted Potato
ingredients_string: Black Pepper:2 | Capers:12 | Dill (Fresh):8 | Garlic (Raw):6 | Leeks:144 | Lemon Juice:36 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):24 | Potato:192 | Preserved Lemon:12 | Rosemary (Fresh):2 | Salmon:328 | Sea Salt:2 | Vegetable Broth (Base):40
short_description: Tender parchment-baked salmon fillets nestled on a bed of sweet sautéed leeks, infused with a bright lemon, caper, and fresh dill reduction. Served alongside crisp, herb-roasted potato cubes.
instructions:
Preheat your oven to 200°C.
Toss cubed potatoes with 5g of olive oil, minced raw garlic, chopped fresh rosemary, and sea salt. Spread onto a baking sheet and roast for 25 minutes until golden.
Heat 10g of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Trim and julienne the leeks, then sauté for 6 to 8 minutes until lightly golden.
Cut out four 40 cm squares of greaseproof paper. Divide half of the sautéed leeks onto the center of each paper square.
Place one salmon fillet over each leek bed. Season lightly with black pepper. Top with finely sliced preserved lemon, capers, and chopped fresh dill.
Spoon 1 tablespoon (15g) of Vegetable Broth (Base) and lemon juice over each fillet, then top with the remaining leeks.
Fold the sides of the paper securely to trap the steam. Bake on a tray for 15 minutes. Garnish with fresh dill and serve immediately alongside the roasted potatoes.

meal_name: Vegan Butternut Squash, Lentil & Nut Stew w Brown Rice
ingredients_string: Almond Butter:11 | Basmati Rice (Brown):45 | Bell Pepper (Red):40 | Black Pepper:1 | Butternut Squash:70 | Cabbage (Purple):15 | Chili Flakes:1 | Coriander Seeds:2 | Garlic (Raw):9 | Lentils (Red):22 | Lime Juice:10 | Mushrooms:35 | Olive Oil:3 | Sea Salt:1 | Spinach (Fresh):20 | Tomato (Raw):60 | Vegetable Stock:150 | Walnuts:8 | Zucchini:35
short_description: A rich and hearty plant-based stew featuring tender butternut squash, red lentils, and creamy almond butter served over wholesome brown rice.
instructions:
"Step 1: Add the finely chopped onion and olive oil to a large saucepan and fry for 5 minutes until soft, then grate in the garlic and stir.
Step 2: Add the chopped tomatoes and cook for a couple of minutes before pouring in 3 cups of water, rinsed red lentils, red pepper, and cubed butternut squash. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Step 3: Stir in the vegetable stock, almond butter, and chili flakes, blending thoroughly until the sauce is smooth and completely combined.
Step 4: Add the cubed zucchini and simmer gently for 20 minutes until the squash and lentils are completely tender.
Step 5: Stir in the chopped mushrooms and the greens (spinach and cabbage), simmering for an additional 2 to 3 minutes until the greens are completely wilted.
Step 6: Season well with sea salt and black pepper. Divide into serving portions over cooked brown rice, and garnish with fresh coriander, crushed walnuts, and a squeeze of fresh lime juice."

meal_name: Tamarind Honey & Sesame Chicken w Garlicky Green Beans
ingredients_string: Broccoli:60 | Chicken thigh:100 | Cucumber Pickle (Base):30 | Garlic (Raw):6 | Ginger (Raw):3 | Green Beans:80 | Honey (Raw):5 | Rice Vinegar:4 | Sea Salt:1 | Sesame Oil:2 | Sesame Seeds:2 | Spring Onion:20 | Tamarind Paste:18
short_description: Juicy oven-baked chicken thighs glazed in a sweet-and-tangy tamarind honey glaze, served with crisp garlicky green beans and steamed broccoli.
instructions:
In a bowl, whisk the tamarind paste, honey, ginger, rice vinegar, crushed garlic, and sea salt with 1g of sesame oil.
Coat the chicken thigh evenly in the marinade, mix in the 5cm pieces of spring onion, and marinate.
Preheat oven to 200°C. Bake chicken and spring onions on a tray for 25 minutes until cooked through.
Lightly steam the broccoli and green beans until tender-crisp, then toss with the remaining 1g of sesame oil and a touch of raw garlic.
Serve the glazed chicken topped with sesame seeds and fresh spring onions alongside the green beans, broccoli, and cucumber pickle.

meal_name: Harissa Chicken Balls w Couscous Cucumber Herb Salad & Eggplant Dip
ingredients_string: Beef Ground:85 | Black Pepper:1 | Couscous:30 | Cucumber:60 | Dill (Fresh):3 | Eggplant Dip (Mutabal) (Base):40 | Fresh Mint:3 | Garlic (Raw):6 | Harissa Paste:15 | Lemon Juice:15 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Parsley:5 | Red Onion:8 | Sea Salt:1 | White Onion:15
short_description: Zesty, pan-seared harissa ground chicken meatballs served alongside a refreshing diced cucumber-herb salad, fluffy couscous, and a creamy, smoky mutabal eggplant dip.
instructions:
"Step 1: In a medium mixing bowl, combine the ground chicken meat, harissa paste, minced garlic, finely chopped white onion, parsley, dill, mint, sea salt, and black pepper. Mix with oiled hands until combined and roll into uniform meatballs.
Step 2: Heat a cast-iron pan over medium-high heat with olive oil. Sear the meatballs for 3-4 minutes per side until deeply golden brown, then transfer to a baking sheet and bake at 400°F for 12 minutes until cooked through.
Step 3: Toss the diced seedless cucumber, fresh lemon juice, finely chopped red onion, fresh dill, mint, sea salt, and black pepper together in a clean bowl to assemble the herb salad.
Step 4: Prepare the couscous according to system guidelines until light and fluffy.
Step 5: Assemble the meal bowls by setting down a base of warm couscous, placing the cooked harissa meatballs alongside, adding a generous portion of the crisp cucumber salad, and serving with a side of the smoky Eggplant Dip (Mutabal) (Base).
Step 6: Garnish the entire plate with a sprinkle of fresh soft herbs, a final light drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, and fresh lemon wedges."

meal_name: Vegan Smoky Cauliflower & Lentil Stew w Quinoa Bread & Tahini
ingredients_string: Carrots:30 | Cauliflower:100 | Cherry Tomatoes:38 | Chili Flakes:1 | Coconut Cream:4 | Coriander Seeds:1 | Cumin Seeds:1 | Garlic (Raw):3 | Lemon Juice:8 | Lentils (Red):97 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Sea Salt:1 | Smoked Paprika:1 | Tahini:12 | Tamari:1 | Tomato Paste:4 | Vegan Worcestershire Sauce:1 | Vegetable Broth (Base):206 | White Onion:28
instructions:
Heat 10g of olive oil (Extra Virgin) in a large saucepan over medium heat. Sauté the chopped white onion until soft and lightly browned.
Add the diced carrots and sliced raw garlic; cook for an additional 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in the cumin seeds, smoked paprika, ground coriander seeds, and chili flakes, toasting for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the cauliflower florets and sliced cherry tomatoes, cooking for 2 minutes.
Pour in the Vegetable Stock, rinsed red lentils, tomato paste, tamari, and Worcestershire sauce. Cover and simmer on low-medium heat for 15 minutes, or until the lentils and cauliflower are tender.
Remove from heat and stir in the coconut cream.
Prepare the dressing by blending the tahini, lemon juice, remaining 8g of olive oil, sea salt, and a splash of filtered water until completely creamy.
Divide into 4 equal portions, drizzle with the tahini dressing, and serve immediately.

meal_name: Salted Caramel Chocolate Bar
ingredients_string: Almond Flour:200 | Coconut Oil:50 | Dark Chocolate Chips:100 | Date Syrup:30 | Sea Salt:10 | Tahini:75
short_description: The most popular dessert on the menu
instructions:
Preheat oven. Line an 8x8 inch square pan with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl add the almond flour, coconut oil, date syrup, vanilla extract and salt. Mix together with a fork until it forms a nice thick crumb texture. Add to the pan and use your fingers to evenly press down into the pan. Bake for 10 minutes. Allow the crust to cool for 10 minutes before adding caramel.
Next make the salted tahini caramel: Add the tahini, date syrup, coconut oil, vanilla extract and sea salt to a medium pot and place over medium-low heat for approximately 2 minutes, stirring frequently. Pour over the slightly cooled crust.
Place in the fridge for at least 30 minutes-1 hour to harden the caramel.
After 30 minutes, make the chocolate layer: melt the chocolate and coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Pour over the caramel layer and tilt the pan side-to-side to evenly distribute the melted chocolate. Place in the fridge for an hour until chocolate is completely hardened and bars are cooled. Remove bars from the pan, sprinkle with sea salt and cut into 16 squares.

meal_name: Chocolate Orange Brownie (N)
ingredients_string: Almond Flour:192 | Baking Powder:5 | Cocoa Powder:86 | Dark Chocolate Chips:50 | Date Syrup:80 | Eggs (Large):100 | Olive Oil:54 | Orange Juice:120 | Orange Zest:12
instructions: A rich, flourless dessert combining deep cocoa flavors with fresh orange zest and juice.

meal_name: Vegan Harissa Roasted Cauliflower & Chickpea Salad w Tahini Dressing
ingredients_string: Black Pepper:1 | Cauliflower:150 | Chickpeas:75 | Dill (Fresh):2 | Fresh Mint:3 | Harissa Paste:15 | Lemon-Tahini Dressing (Base):35 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):5 | Sea Salt:1 | Shallots:20
short_description: Smoky harissa-roasted cauliflower florets and crispy chickpeas tossed with sweet sliced shallots, fresh soft herbs, and a velvety lemon-tahini dressing.
instructions:
"Step 1: Preheat the oven to 425°F (218°C). Spread the cut cauliflower florets across a large rimmed baking sheet and toss evenly with 1 tablespoon of extra-virgin olive oil, 1 tablespoon of harissa paste, and a pinch of sea salt.
Step 2: Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes until the edges start to brown.
Step 3: Remove the baking sheet from the oven, toss the florets, and brush with the remaining harissa paste. Push the cauliflower tightly to one side of the pan.
Step 4: Add the rinsed, drained chickpeas and sliced shallots to the cleared side of the sheet. Toss them cleanly with the remaining olive oil, sea salt, and black pepper.
Step 5: Return the baking sheet to the oven and roast for an additional 15 to 20 minutes until the cauliflower edges turn deeply caramelized and the chickpeas are crisp.
Step 6: While roasting finishes, combine all dressing ingredients in a bowl, slowly whisking in warm water until a creamy emulsion forms.
Step 7: Transfer the warm roasted components into a clean mixing bowl, toss gently with fresh torn mint and dill, and drizzle evenly with the completed Lemon-Tahini Dressing."

meal_name: Eggplant & Ground Beef Stew w Quinoa Bread
ingredients_string: Avocado Oil:4 | Beef Ground:80 | Black Pepper:1 | Chili Flakes:1 | Eggplant:90 | Garlic (Raw):5 | Ginger (Raw):3 | Parsley:2 | Quinoa Bread (Base):45 | Sea Salt:1 | Sesame Oil:3 | Soy Sauce:8 | White Onion:20
short_description: A savory, deeply comforting skillet stir-fry of tender browned eggplant cubes and crumbled lean ground beef tossed with aromatics, sesame oil, and soy sauce, paired with a slice of rustic quinoa bread.
instructions:
"Step 1: Heat a portion of the avocado oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the uniform eggplant cubes and stir-fry continuously for 5 to 7 minutes until they soften and turn golden brown, then remove from pan and set aside.
Step 2: In the same skillet, add the remaining avocado oil and input the ground beef. Cook for 5 to 8 minutes until entirely browned and crumbly, fracturing it apart with a spatula as it cooks.
Step 3: Introduce the finely chopped white onion, minced raw garlic, and grated ginger into the pan with the beef, stir-frying for 2 to 3 minutes until fragrant.
Step 4: Return the browned eggplant cubes to the skillet alongside the diced bell peppers and toss thoroughly to combine.
Step 5: Pour in the soy sauce and sesame oil, sprinkling in the optional chili flakes. Stir-fry the mixture over medium heat for an additional 3 to 5 minutes until all components are completely coated and flavors emulsify.
Step 6: Season with sea salt and black pepper to taste, remove from heat source, and serve hot garnished with fresh chopped soft herbs alongside a sliced portion of toasted Quinoa Bread (Base)."

meal_name: Vegan Mushroom Bowl
ingredients_string: Bell Pepper (Red):45 | Broccoli:50 | Cherry Tomato:40 | Cilantro Lime Dressing (Base):22 | Fresh Coriander:3 | Garlic (Raw):3 | Mushrooms:110 | Quinoa:35 | Spinach (Fresh):25 | White Onion:20
short_description: A vibrant, nutrient-dense plant-based bowl featuring earthy sautéed Portobello mushrooms, fluffy quinoa, tender broccoli, and sweet whole-roasted cherry tomatoes over a bed of wilted spinach, served with a zesty cilantro lime dressing.
instructions:
"Step 1: Heat a small portion of the oil from your dressing allotment in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the sliced Portobello mushrooms and sauté for 3 to 4 minutes until they begin to brown.
Step 2: Introduce the finely chopped white onion, minced garlic, and diced red bell peppers into the skillet. Sauté for an additional 3 minutes until the vegetables turn soft and translucent.
Step 3: Toss the whole cherry tomatoes lightly in oil and roast them on a baking sheet at 400°F (204°C) for 10 to 12 minutes until their skins blister and split.
Step 4: Add the bite-sized broccoli florets and fresh spinach leaves directly into the skillet with the mushrooms, stirring gently for 2 to 3 minutes until the broccoli turns bright green and the spinach wilts down cleanly.
Step 5: Assemble the meal bowls by laying down a base of warm, fluffy pre-cooked quinoa. Arrange the sautéed mushrooms and vegetables neatly over the top, then crown with the blistered roasted cherry tomatoes.
Step 6: Garnish the finished bowl with a sprinkle of fresh chopped coriander and sliced spring onions, serving the emulsified Cilantro Lime Dressing (Base) securely on the side."

meal_name: Spicy Harissa Grilled Chicken w Roasted Sweet Potato & Zucchini
ingredients_string: Black Pepper:1 | Chicken Breast:110 | Garlic (Raw):3 | Harissa Paste (Base):20 | Lemon Juice:10 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Sea Salt:1 | Sweet Potato:90 | Zucchini:80
short_description: Lean chicken breast marinated in our house-made spicy harissa paste base, grilled to juicy perfection, and served with caramelized roasted sweet potato wedges and tender sliced zucchini.
instructions:
"Step 1: In a small bowl, whisk together the Harissa Paste (Base), fresh lemon juice, minced raw garlic, and half of the olive oil to create a uniform marinade.
Step 2: Coat the lean chicken breast evenly in the harissa marinade and let it rest for at least 15 minutes to infuse the spicy profiles completely.
Step 3: Preheat your oven to 400°F (204°C). Toss the cut sweet potato wedges and sliced zucchini in the remaining olive oil, sea salt, and black pepper.
Step 4: Spread the vegetables evenly across a baking sheet and roast for 20 to 25 minutes until the sweet potatoes are tender and lightly browned at the edges.
Step 5: Heat a grill pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Grill the marinated chicken breast for 5 to 6 minutes per side until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Step 6: Allow the grilled chicken to rest for 3 minutes before slicing. Arrange the sliced chicken on a plate alongside the roasted sweet potatoes and zucchini, serving immediately."

meal_name: Grilled Beef Steak Ratatouille & Saffron rice
ingredients_string: Beef Sirloin:90 | Black Pepper:1 | Cajun Powder:2 | Lemon Juice:10 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):5 | Parsley:5 | Ratatouille (Base):120 | Saffron Rice (Base):70 | Sea Salt:1
short_description: Seared sweet-paprika rubbed lean beef steak sliced over an herb-mustard board dressing, served alongside rich, slow-simmered harissa-anchovy ratatouille and aromatic golden saffron basmati rice.
instructions:
"Step 1: Prepare the custom Ratatouille (Base) and Saffron Rice (Base) profiles according to internal library specifications and hold warm.
Step 2: Rub the beef sirloin steak evenly with sweet paprika (Cajun powder), salt, and black pepper.
Step 3: Heat a grill pan or cast-iron skillet over high heat with a light touch of olive oil and sear the steak for 3 to 4 minutes per side, or until cooked to your preferred doneness.
Step 4: On a clean chopping board, finely slice the fresh parsley stalks and chop the leaves. Mix directly on the board with Dijon mustard, extra virgin olive oil, and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, spreading it out into a flat dressing layer.
Step 5: Transfer the hot grilled steak directly onto the board dressing, turning it over to coat all sides thoroughly, and allow it to rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Step 6: Plate a clean mound of bright golden saffron rice alongside a generous serving of savory ratatouille, top with the sliced board-dressed steak, and serve immediately."

meal_name: Smashed white Beans
ingredients_string: Black Pepper:5 | Cannellini Beans:1000 | Garlic (Raw):10 | Lemon Juice:80 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):100 | Sea Salt:15 | Water (Filtered):3000
instructions:
The Soak (Essential for Texture)
Place the 1kg of dry beans in a large bowl and cover with 3–4 liters of water.
Soak for at least 8 to 12 hours. This ensures even cooking and better digestibility.
Drain and rinse thoroughly.
The Gentle Simmer
Place the soaked beans in a large pot and cover with 3 liters of fresh water.
Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce to a very low simmer. Skim off any foam that rises to the top.
Cook for 45 to 90 minutes (time varies by bean age). You want them tender enough to mash easily but not falling apart.
Crucial: Do not add the salt or lemon juice until the beans are fully cooked, as acid and salt can toughen the skins during the boiling process.
The Infusion
Once the beans are tender, drain them (reserve 1 cup of the cooking liquid just in case).
While the beans are still hot, stir in the 1 tablespoon of sautéed garlic and onion.
Add the sea salt, pepper, and lemon juice.
The Emulsion
Slowly pour in the olive oil while gently folding the beans. The heat from the beans will emulsify the oil and lemon juice, creating a light, silky coating.
If you want a "mash" texture for your breakfasts, use a potato masher to crush roughly 20% of the beans while they are warm.

meal_name: Marinated Pineapple, Peppers, Red Onion & Cilantro Side Salad
ingredients_string: Avocado:20 | Bell Pepper (Red):25 | Cabbage (Purple):45 | Cucumber:35 | Fresh Coriander:4 | Pineapple:40 | Red Onion:12 | Red Thai Chillies:2 | Zesty Lime Chili Salad Dressing (Base):12
short_description: A tropical, crisp, and refreshing raw side salad combining juicy pineapple chunks, creamy avocado, crunchy diced peppers, halfmoon cucumbers, and shredded cabbages, all tossed in a fiery lime-chili vinaigrette.
instructions:
"Step 1: Slice the white and purple cabbages into fine shreds, cut the deseeded cucumber into clean halfmoon shapes, and dice the fresh red bell pepper, red onion, and seeded chili pepper.
Step 2: Chop the fresh pineapple flesh into uniform, bite-sized chunks and dice the creamy avocado flesh right before assembling to prevent oxidation.
Step 3: Combine the shredded cabbages, cucumber halfmoons, diced bell peppers, onion slices, minced chili, and pineapple chunks together in a large mixing bowl.
Step 4: Drizzle the pre-emulsified Zesty Lime Chili Salad Dressing (Base) directly over the salad bowl components, tossing everything gently to coat the raw elements evenly without breaking the fruit pieces.
Step 5: Fold in the freshly diced avocado cubes and finely chopped coriander leaves at the very end.
Step 6: Allow the salad to sit marinated for 5 minutes before plating to let the citrus acids lightly soften the onion strings and meld with the tropical pineapple juices."

meal_name: Roasted Chicken in Pomegranate & Sumac Sauce w Turmeric Rice
ingredients_string: Black Pepper:1 | Chicken Breast:115 | Parsley:3 | Pomegranate Seeds:10 | Pomegranate Sumac Sauce (Base):30 | Red Onion:40 | Sea Salt:1 | Turmeric Rice (Base):70
short_description: Tender oven-roasted chicken breast marinated in a tart pomegranate, sumac, and fresh herb reduction, served over caramelized red onions and a bed of vibrant golden turmeric basmati rice, finished with fresh ruby pomegranate seeds.
instructions:
"Step 1: Toss the chicken breast thoroughly in the pre-prepared Pomegranate Sumac Sauce (Base), massaging the garlic and Thyme (Fresh) sprigs directly into the meat, and let it marinate in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Step 2: Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). Thinly slice the fresh red onion and layer the strings evenly across the bottom of a clean ceramic roasting dish.
Step 3: Arrange the marinated chicken pieces neatly over the bed of red onions, pouring all remaining pomegranate sumac marinade directly over the top, and season with an extra pinch of sea salt and ground black pepper.
Step 4: Roast in the oven for 45 to 50 minutes, open the door every 15 minutes to spoon the dark crimson pan juices back over the chicken to baste it and keep the meat intensely moist.
Step 5: Cook your golden Turmeric Rice (Base) according to library specifications and plate a clean serving mound.
Step 6: Slice the juicy roasted chicken breast and lay it over the caramelized onions alongside the rice. Spoon the concentrated pan reduction glaze over the top, and garnish with fresh chopped parsley and bright ruby pomegranate seeds."

meal_name: Vegan Sri Lankan Red Lentil Dal w Quinoa Bread
ingredients_string: Chili Dried:1 | Chili Flakes:1 | Chilli Powder:1 | Coconut Milk:25 | Garlic (Raw):4 | Ginger:2 | Lentils (Red):40 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Quinoa Flatbread (Base):70 | Sea Salt:2 | Tomato (Raw):20 | Turmeric (Ground):1 | White Onion:20 | coriander powder:1 | cumin powder:1 | mustard seeds:1
short_description: Rich, slow-simmered Sri Lankan red lentil dal infused with creamy coconut milk and traditional spices, topped with a sizzling mustard seed and dried chili tempering, served with a warm house-made quinoa flatbread.
instructions:
"Step 1: Rinse the Lentils (Red) thoroughly under cold running water until the liquid runs completely clear.
Step 2: In a small pot, combine the rinsed red lentils, 1 cup of water, chopped white onion, chopped tomato, sliced garlic, minced ginger, Turmeric Ground, Chilli Powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and sea salt. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over high heat.
Step 3: Reduce heat to medium-low, cover with a lid, and simmer for 12 to 15 minutes until the lentils are tender and the water is completely absorbed.
Step 4: Pour in the coconut milk along with 1/4 cup of filtered water, stir gently to combine, and let the dal simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes until thick and creamy.
Step 5: Prepare the tempering oil by heating the extra virgin olive oil in a small separate pan over medium heat. Add the mustard seeds, cumin powder, Chili Dried, and chili flakes, cooking for 1 to 2 minutes until the seeds begin to pop and crackle.
Step 6: Pour the sizzling spiced tempering oil directly over the top of the cooked lentil dal and serve immediately inside a deep bowl alongside a warm, fresh piece of your pre-baked Quinoa Flatbread (Base)."

meal_name: Tomato Parsely Salad w Sumac Za’ater Dressing
ingredients_string: Fresh Mint:2 | Parsley:10 | Red Onion:13 | Sumac Za'atar Dressing (Base):31 | Tomato (Raw):165
short_description: A vibrant, refreshing side salad featuring halved plum tomatoes and sliced red onions tossed with fresh parsley and mint in a house-made, zesty sumac za'atar dressing base.
instructions:
"1. Prepare the Sumac Za'atar Dressing (Base) ahead of time.
Put tomato halves, onion slices, and chopped herbs into a large bowl.
Measure and pour 31g of the prepared Sumac Za'atar Dressing (Base) over the salad components.
Toss gently to combine completely before serving."

meal_name: Apple Pie Balls
ingredients_string: Almond Butter:20 | Apple Green:10 | Cinnamon:1 | Date Syrup:21 | Oats (Rolled):30 | Sea Salt:1 | Vanilla Extract:1
short_description: A clean, no-bake whole-food dessert ball combining rolled oats, warm cinnamon, and fresh grated green apple, bound naturally with creamy almond butter and dark date syrup.
instructions:
"1. In a medium bowl, combine the grated green apple, almond butter, date syrup, and vanilla extract, and stir until completely smooth.
Add the rolled oats, cinnamon, and sea salt, and mix until evenly distributed.
Chill the unified mixture in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to firm up.
Use a tablespoon to scoop out the portion, then roll into compact balls with your hands."

meal_name: Garlic Lemon Salmon, Dill Tarragon Rice & Spicy Green Chilli Sauce
ingredients_string: Basmati Rice (White):35 | Black Pepper:1 | Dill (Fresh):10 | Garlic (Raw):6 | Lemon Juice:15 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Salmon (Raw):130 | Spicy Green Chilli Sauce (Base):15
short_description: A tender garlic-lemon salmon fillet served over fluffy, fragrant dill and tarragon rice with a fiery house-made green chilli sauce on the side.
instructions:
"1. Cook basmati rice according to package directions; fluff and fold in chopped fresh dill and dried tarragon.
In a small bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, chopped garlic, oregano, sweet paprika, and black pepper.
Arrange salmon fillet in a baking dish, coat thoroughly with the garlic-lemon marinade, and top with lemon slices.
Bake at 200°C for 12 to 15 minutes until the salmon flakes easily.
Plate the cooked salmon over the dill tarragon rice.
Garnish with lemon slices and freshly chopped parsley, and serve with 15g of the prepared Spicy Green Chilli Sauce (Base) on the side."

meal_name: Blackened Salmon w Sun Dried Tomato, Green Peas & Turmeric Rice
ingredients_string: Bell Pepper (Red):15 | Dill (Fresh):2 | Green Peas:20 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Salmon (Raw):130 | Sun-dried Tomato:10 | Turmeric Rice (Base):120
instructions:
"1. Prepare the Turmeric Rice (Base) by heating olive oil in a skillet and sautéing diced onion, bell pepper, and sun-dried tomatoes for 5 minutes.
Add the rice and toast for 2 minutes, then stir in the coconut milk, vegetable broth, green peas, turmeric, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a simmer, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook for 15-20 minutes until the liquid is fully absorbed and rice is tender.
Meanwhile, season the salmon fillet with blackening spices and sear or grill to perfection.
Plate 120g of the finished seasoned turmeric rice base, place the blackened salmon fillet on top, and garnish with fresh dill and grilled lemon wedges."

meal_name: Pepper Chicken in Creamy Cajun Sauce w Roasted Potato
ingredients_string: Bell Pepper (Red):40 | Cajun Powder:2 | Cherry Tomatoes:30 | Chicken Breast:120 | Chicken Broth:50 | Coconut Cream:20 | Garlic (Raw):4 | Lime Juice:5 | Olive Oil (Extra Virgin):4 | Potato:90 | Red Onion:15 | Smoked Paprika:2 | Tapioca Starch:3
short_description: A bold, skillet-seared Cajun blackened chicken breast smothered in a luxurious, non-dairy coconut cream sauce with fire-roasted cherry tomatoes and bell peppers, paired with clean roasted potato cubes.
instructions:
"1. Brush chicken breast lightly with olive oil and coat evenly with 2/3 of the smoked paprika, cajun powder, salt, and pepper mix.
Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken for 4 minutes until a blackened crust forms, flip, and cook 4-5 minutes until done; set chicken aside.
In the same skillet, add a splash of chicken broth to deglaze. Sauté diced onions, bell peppers, and minced garlic with the remaining spice mix for 2 minutes.
Whisk the remaining chicken broth with tapioca starch until completely smooth, then pour into the skillet along with the coconut cream and drained cherry tomatoes.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer until it thickens smoothly.
Return the chicken to the skillet, finish with lime juice, and coat with the cream sauce and sautéed peppers. Serve warm with clean oven-roasted potato cubes."

meal_name: Cajun Chicken, Grilled Peppers & Onion Salad w Quinoa, Kale & Mustard Dressing
ingredients_string: Bell Pepper (Red):40 | Cajun Powder:1 | Chicken Breast:120 | Garlic (Raw):2 | Kale:40 | Mustard Dressing (Base):15 | Quinoa (Base):80 | Red Onion:20 | Smoked Paprika:1
short_description: A dynamic, protein-packed warm salad featuring sliced blackened Cajun chicken breast, grilled bell peppers, and red onion layered over fluffy quinoa and massaged kale, tossed in a zesty house-made mustard oil dressing base.
instructions:
"1. Rub the chicken breast evenly with 1g smoked paprika, 1g cajun powder, minced garlic, sea salt, and black pepper.
Heat a heavy pan or grill over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 5 minutes per side until charred and cooked through; rest and slice into thin strips.
In the same pan, lightly char the thick-cut bell pepper strips and red onion slices for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Weigh out 80g of pre-prepared Quinoa (Base) and place it in your meal assembly bowl.
Add the raw kale, massaging it slightly to soften, then toss with the quinoa, grilled peppers, and red onions.
Top the salad base with the sliced Cajun chicken.
Measure and drizzle exactly 15g of the pre-prepared Mustard Dressing (Base) over the final assembly and serve warm."

meal_name: Grilled Rosemary Garlic Chicken Salad w Rocca & Red Pepper Dressing
ingredients_string: Parmesan:5 | Red Pepper Dressing (Base):20 | Roasted Cherry Tomato (Base):40 | Rocca:40 | Rosemary Garlic Chicken (Base):95 | Walnuts:10
instructions:
"1. Weigh out 40g of fresh, crisp rocca greens and arrange them beautifully as the base of your salad bowl.
Scatter 40g of pre-prepared Roasted Cherry Tomato (Base) evenly over the greens.
Slice 95g of pre-cooked Rosemary Garlic Chicken (Base) and layer it across the top of the salad.
Evenly distribute 10g of raw walnut halves and sprinkle 5g of finely shaved parmesan cheese over the dish.
Drizzle exactly 20g of the emulsified Red Pepper Dressing (Base) over the salad components immediately before serving warm."

meal_name: Baked Salmon with Fermented Chimichurri & Steamed Basmati Rice
ingredients_string: Fermented Chimichurri (Base):25 | Fresh Coriander:2 | Salmon:125 | Steamed Basmati Rice (Base):90
short_description: A premium baked salmon fillet generously blanketed in an aromatic, aged fermented chimichurri herb sauce, served alongside a clean mound of fluffy Steamed Basmati Rice (Base).
instructions:
"1. Weigh out exactly 90g of pre-prepared, warm Steamed Basmati Rice (Base) and place it on the serving plate.
Preheat the oven to 190°C.
Place the 125g clean salmon fillet on a parchment-lined baking tray.
Spoon exactly 15g of your house-made Fermented Chimichurri (Base) over the top surface of the fish, coating it smoothly.
Bake for 12-14 minutes until tender and flashing clean flakes under a fork.
Plate the salmon next to the basmati rice mound.
Dress the fish with the remaining 10g of your raw fermented herb sauce, garnish with fresh coriander sprigs, and serve immediately."
